title: Seeking a pipe with SeekFrom::End silently returns a generic error instead of explaining forward-only limitation
body: input.rs's Input::seek() calls try_skip() which returns Err via cant_skip_abs_err() for SeekFrom::Start/SeekFrom::End on a pipe, but the io::Error::other message only says 'Pipes only support seeking forward with a relative offset' without echoing which SeekFrom variant was attempted, making it harder to tell -n/--length interactions from -s/--skip issues when piping input.

title: HEXYL_COLOR_* env var falls back to default silently on invalid color string
body: colors.rs's init_color() matches DynColors::from_str(color) and falls back to `default` on any parse failure without printing a warning, so setting HEXYL_COLOR_NULL=not-a-color just quietly reverts to bright-black with no indication anything was wrong with the env var.

title: COLOR_GRADIENT_NONASCII / ASCII gradients are computed at compile time but not configurable via CLI
body: colors.rs defines COLOR_GRADIENT_NONASCII, COLOR_GRADIENT_ASCII_NONPRINTABLE, and COLOR_GRADIENT_ASCII_PRINTABLE as const arrays built by generate_color_gradient with hardcoded RGB stops. Unlike COLOR_NULL/COLOR_OFFSET etc. which read HEXYL_COLOR_* env vars, there's no way to customize the gradient endpoints without recompiling hexyl.

title: hexyl panics with 'attempt to subtract with overflow' when --skip is larger than input length on a regular file
body: When file.seek(SeekFrom::Start(n)) succeeds (seeking past EOF is legal for regular files), subsequent length/offset arithmetic in main.rs assumes the requested skip actually landed within the file and can underflow when computing remaining bytes to print, causing a debug-mode panic instead of printing zero rows.

title: Document how --border and --squeeze interact for empty (zero-byte) input
body: src/tests.rs and tests/integration_tests.rs cover many flag combinations, but I couldn't find a case for `hexyl --squeezing /dev/null` verifying the border/panel output for zero-byte input -- the README doesn't describe expected output shape here either.
